page: Microsoft Azure D2plds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine belonging to the DPLDSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It is equipped with 77 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the server delivers moderate single-core capabilities but struggles with multi-core and memory-intensive workloads. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated as average at 1821.98, and single-threaded gzip compression reaches an average of 24.16 GB/sec. However, multi-core stress-ng performance is weak at 3613.27, and Geekbench scores are low, with a single-core score of 1095.0 and a multi-core score of 2010.0. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with uncached read and write speeds at 26.37 GB/sec and 16.5 GB/sec respectively, which limits data-heavy operations.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D2plds_v5 offers a low-cost entry point into ARM-based cloud computing, trading off high-performance memory and multi-threaded execution for dedicated, power-efficient CPU cores. This virtual machine is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, simple text processing, and development or testing environments that do not require extensive memory or high-throughput database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D2plds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine from the DPLDSv5 family, powered by a 2-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 4.0 GB of RAM, 77 GB of local storage, and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmarks show average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1821.98 and gzip compression of 24.16 GB/sec, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server represents a low-cost, resource-constrained option. It is best suited for lightweight workloads, basic web serving, and simple single-threaded applications.

tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine with dedicated cores and local storage, optimized for lightweight, single-threaded utility workloads.

Includes 4.0 GB of system memory and 77 GB of local storage
Delivers average single-core CPU and single-threaded gzip compression performance
Shows weak multi-core scaling and constrained memory bandwidth in benchmarks
Suited for lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and development tasks
